title: Use `write` instead of `stream.write` in `PrettyPrinter._pprint_frozendict`
author: devdanzin
body:
# Bug report

### Bug description:

In the recent PR that added `frozendict` support to `pprint` (https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/144908), I let a small inconsistency get through: we set `write = stream.write` then immediately uses `stream.write` two lines below:

https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/cd5217283112d41c0244e2d96302cbe33f0b4cb1/Lib/pprint.py#L238-L248

cc @vstinner. May I write the trivial PR?

### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

<!-- gh-linked-prs -->
### Linked PRs
* gh-145894
<!-- /gh-linked-prs -->

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
Ah right, `write()` should be used instead of `stream.write`.

> May I write the trivial PR?

Sure, go ahead.
